name: WS2812B-Mini (3535)
designNotes: Top-mount LED -- shines upward, suitable for per-key backlighting with transparent keycaps
Higher current draw than SK6812-MINI-E (36mA vs 15mA per LED at full brightness)
Significant battery drain impact -- not ideal for wireless keyboards unless dimmed
3535 package is smaller than standard WS2812B (5050) but still top-mount
Requires 100nF decoupling cap per LED close to VDD/GND pins
3.3V logic from nRF52840 may not reliably drive data line -- use level shifter or 74HCT245
For underglow-only use, SK6812-MINI-E reverse-mount is generally preferred
summary: Top-mount addressable RGB LED in compact 3535 package — for per-key backlighting
features: Top-mount for per-key backlighting through transparent keycaps
Available at LCSC for JLCPCB assembly
concerns: High current draw at 36mA per LED — drains batteries quickly
Not recommended for wireless keyboards unless heavily dimmed
3.3V logic may not reliably drive data line — level shifter recommended
100nF decoupling cap required per LED near VDD/GND
Top-mount adds height — not ideal for ultra-low-profile builds
